slides.title: How Small Businesses Win with Claude AI

layout.reserved_note: Presenter video overlay — leave this region completely empty, no background elements or text bleed into it

content.headline:
How Small
Businesses Win
with Claude AI
content.subheadline: The gap between where you are and where you could be has never been smaller.

chart.data_description: 8-point upward-trending curve. Starts mid-low, dips slightly to its lowest point at index 3 (the 'You Are Here' marker), then climbs with accelerating momentum to a high point at index 7 (the 'AI-Native' marker).

annotations.trigger_note: Both annotations appear simultaneously on first spacebar press. A second spacebar press advances to the next slide (standard deck navigation).

arrow.direction: pointing down-right toward circle from upper-left
pointing down-left toward circle from upper-right

slides.prompt: Build a single full-viewport HTML slide following DESIGN.md. The slide is divided into two columns: LEFT 8/12 holds all content, RIGHT 4/12 is completely empty — no backgrounds, borders, or elements of any kind (reserved for presenter video overlay). In the left region, render from top: a thin 120px terracotta rule line (#C8744A), then a large display headline in Fraunces weight-300 reading 'How Small Businesses Win with Claude AI' (break after 'Small' and 'Businesses'), then a subtitle in DM Sans reading 'The gap between where you are and where you could be has never been smaller.', then below that a 160px tall SVG line chart. The chart has 8 data points: [0.55, 0.48, 0.52, 0.45, 0.50, 0.62, 0.75, 0.92] — the lowest point is index 3 and the highest is index 7. Render the chart with a smooth cubic bezier curve in #C8744A (2.5px stroke), soft orange area fill with a gradient from #C8744A44 to transparent, no axes, no grid, no labels. Animate the line drawing left-to-right on page load over 800ms ease-out. On the first spacebar keypress, simultaneously reveal two annotations over the chart: (1) At the index-3 low point — an open circle ring (8px radius, #C8744A stroke, transparent fill), a short angled arrow pointing down-right toward the circle from upper-left, and the label 'YOU ARE HERE' in DM Mono 12px uppercase #F0EBE3 above the arrow. (2) At the index-7 high point — a filled circle (8px radius, #C8744A fill), a short angled arrow pointing down-left toward the circle from upper-right, and the label 'AI-NATIVE' in DM Mono 12px uppercase #C8744A above the arrow. Both annotations fade in and scale from 0.8 to 1 over 300ms ease-out; the high-point annotation has a 120ms delay. Background is #0E0D0C. All tokens and spacing follow DESIGN.md exactly.

slides_next.title: The Cost of Doing It Manually

layout.reserved_note: Presenter video overlay — completely empty, no backgrounds, borders, or elements

content.headline:
You're spending
time you don't have
on work that doesn't scale.
content.headline_note: Display font (Fraunces), weight 300. Line breaks as shown.

time_waste_block.description: A horizontal bar showing a visual breakdown of a typical 40-hour work week. The bar is divided into two segments: 'Manual Work' takes up 40% of the bar (filled in terracotta #C8744A), and 'Everything Else' takes the remaining 60% (filled in --color-border #2A2724). Below the bar, a single stark stat: '15+ hours/week on tasks AI can handle'.
time_waste_block.stat_sublabel: on tasks AI can handle — follow-ups, reporting, scheduling, data entry

bar.animation: segments fill left-to-right on load, staggered 200ms apart

steps.label: Reveal opportunity reframe
steps.description: The manual work segment of the bar pulses briefly (scale 1.0 → 1.02 → 1.0, 400ms), then a callout block fades up below the bar. The callout asks the rhetorical question and shows the calculator.

steps.label: Reveal annual total
steps.description: The dollar total counts up from $0 to the final value using an animated counter, 800ms ease-out. A terracotta underline rule grows beneath it.

opportunity_block.rhetorical_question:
What if those 15 hours
gave you one more client a month?

calculator.description: A clean inline calculator-style display. Two input rows with muted labels on the left and editable values on the right. User can adjust the values to see the annual total update in real time.

underline_rule.width: grows from 0 to 100% of number width

slides_next.prompt: Build a single full-viewport HTML slide following DESIGN.md. LEFT 8/12 holds all content; RIGHT 4/12 is completely empty (presenter video region — no elements, borders, or backgrounds). No spacebar hint. In the left region render: (1) A thin 120px terracotta rule line above the headline. (2) Headline in Fraunces weight-300 44px: 'You're spending time you don't have on work that doesn't scale.' with natural line breaks. (3) A horizontal segmented bar (10px tall, full content width, border-radius 2px). Left 40% fills in terracotta #C8744A on load with a left-to-right fill animation (600ms ease-out). Right 60% is #2A2724. Below the bar, a label in DM Mono 11px uppercase muted: '15+ HRS/WEEK ON TASKS AI CAN HANDLE — FOLLOW-UPS, REPORTING, SCHEDULING, DATA ENTRY'. (4) On FIRST spacebar press: a block fades up below the bar stat label. It contains a rhetorical question in Fraunces italic weight-300 28px: 'What if those 15 hours gave you one more client a month?' Below that, a minimal two-row calculator: row 1 label 'Avg. client value / month' with default value '$2,000'; row 2 label 'Extra clients / month' with default value '1'. Both rows use DM Mono labels (muted, 12px) and DM Sans values (off-white, 20px). Values are editable inputs that recalculate the result live. Below the calculator rows, a result label in DM Mono 11px uppercase muted reads 'POTENTIAL EXTRA REVENUE / YEAR'. (5) On SECOND spacebar press: the dollar result counts up from $0 to $24,000 (recalculated from inputs) in Fraunces weight-300 56px terracotta #C8744A over 800ms ease-out. After the counter finishes, a 1px terracotta underline rule grows left-to-right beneath the number over 400ms. The calculator inputs update the result in real time whenever the user edits them. Background #0E0D0C. All DESIGN.md tokens apply.

slides_next.title: The Three-Step Path

layout.reserved_note: Presenter video overlay — completely empty, no backgrounds, borders, or elements

content.headline:
Three steps to
becoming AI-Native.
content.headline_note: Fraunces weight-300. Line break after 'steps to'.

steps.label: Discovery
steps.description: We map your workflows, find the hours being lost, and identify exactly where AI will drive the fastest return.
steps.entrance: fade-up (translateY 16px → 0, opacity 0 → 1, 400ms ease-out) + left accent bar grows top-to-bottom (height 0 → 100%, 400ms ease-out, simultaneous)

steps.label: Implementation
steps.description: We build and deploy the automations — tested, integrated with your existing tools, and ready from day one.
steps.entrance: fade-up (translateY 16px → 0, opacity 0 → 1, 400ms ease-out) + left accent bar grows top-to-bottom (height 0 → 100%, 400ms ease-out, simultaneous)

steps.label: Adoption
steps.description: We train your team, refine the systems, and make sure the gains compound — not just land and fade.
steps.entrance: fade-up (translateY 16px → 0, opacity 0 → 1, 400ms ease-out) + left accent bar grows top-to-bottom (height 0 → 100%, 400ms ease-out, simultaneous)

left_accent_bar.position: absolute, flush to left inner edge of card
left_accent_bar.height_animation: grows from 0 to 100% of card height on entrance, 400ms ease-out

slides_next.prompt: Build a single full-viewport HTML slide following DESIGN.md. LEFT 8/12 holds all content; RIGHT 4/12 is completely empty (presenter video — no elements, borders, or backgrounds). No spacebar hint anywhere. In the left region render: (1) A thin 120px terracotta rule (#C8744A, 1px, margin-bottom 24px) above the headline. (2) Headline in Fraunces weight-300 44px: 'Three steps to becoming AI-Native.' with a line break after 'to'. (3) Three step cards stacked vertically with 12px gaps, all hidden (opacity 0, pointer-events none) on load. Each card: background #161513, border 1px solid #2A2724, border-radius 4px, padding 20px 24px 20px 28px, position relative, overflow hidden. Each card has a 3px wide colored left accent bar absolutely positioned at the left edge (top 0, left 0, bottom 0), initially height 0, that animates to full card height simultaneously with the card fade-up. Inside each card: step number in DM Mono 11px weight-500 uppercase colored text above the label, then step label in Fraunces weight-300 26px off-white (#F0EBE3), then description in DM Sans 14px muted (#7A7068) below. Step 01 DISCOVERY: accent color #C0392B. Step 02 IMPLEMENTATION: accent color #2980B9. Step 03 ADOPTION: accent color #27AE60. On each spacebar press reveal the next card: fade-up (translateY 16px→0, opacity 0→1) AND left accent bar grows (height 0→100%) both over 400ms ease-out simultaneously. First press: step 01. Second press: step 02. Third press: step 03. No further action on subsequent presses. Background #0E0D0C. All DESIGN.md tokens apply.

slides_next.title: Discovery — Understanding the Objective

layout.reserved_note: Presenter video overlay — completely empty, no backgrounds, borders, or elements

content.headline:
Before we build anything,
we need to understand
the objective.
content.headline_note: Fraunces weight-300. Sets up the diagram reveal below.

diagram.description: A horizontal SVG scene that builds piece by piece across the width of the content area. Each spacebar press adds the next visual element left-to-right. All elements are minimal, icon-style SVG line art in off-white (#F0EBE3) and muted tones, consistent stroke weight (2px). The scene is persistent — each element stays visible once revealed.
diagram.ground_line: a single horizontal baseline rule across the full width at 70% height, color #2A2724, present from load

elements.position: far left of scene, standing on ground line
elements.visual: Minimal stick figure: circle head, single vertical line body, two angled legs, two angled arms slightly raised — all stroked in #F0EBE3, 2px stroke
elements.label: Where you are
elements.label_position: below ground line, centered under figure

elements.position: far right of scene (before reserved video region), standing on ground line
elements.visual: Simple treasure chest: a rectangle body with a rounded-top lid, a horizontal line dividing lid from body, a small circle latch in center, a subtle glow or accent lines suggesting gold — stroked in #C8744A, 2px stroke, fill none
elements.label: Where you want to go
elements.label_position: below ground line, centered under chest

elements.position: center of scene, between stick figure and treasure chest
elements.visual: A gap in the ground line — the baseline breaks in the middle third. Inside the gap, a fire pit: three upward flame shapes drawn in #C0392B and #C8744A, layered and flickering. The gap edges are jagged/rough. The baseline on either side of the gap remains.
elements.label: What's stopping you
elements.entrance: flames flicker in with opacity animation cycling 0.6→1→0.6, gap edges appear with a quick draw-in, 400ms
elements.animation_note: Flames have a gentle looping flicker after reveal: opacity oscillates between 0.7 and 1.0 with a 1.2s ease-in-out loop

elements.position: spanning the gap in the center, connecting both ground edges
elements.visual: A simple flat bridge arc drawn as a path spanning the gap — a shallow curved or flat horizontal line at ground level connecting the two edges over the fire. Two short vertical support lines drop from the bridge edges. Stroked in #F0EBE3, 2px.
elements.label: How to get there
elements.entrance: bridge path draws in left-to-right using stroke-dashoffset animation, 500ms ease-out

elements.visual: The bridge surface fills with small rectangle bricks — 5 to 7 evenly spaced rectangles drawn along the bridge span, each labeled or implied as a component (no text on them, just the brick shapes). Stroked in #C8744A, 1.5px, fill #3D2418 (very subtle warm fill).
elements.label: What makes it a solution
elements.entrance: bricks pop in one by one with staggered 60ms delays, each scaling from 0 to 1, 250ms ease-out

design_tokens.font_eyebrow: DM Mono, 11px, weight 500, uppercase, +0.08em tracking, color #C0392B

slides_next.prompt: Build a single full-viewport HTML slide following DESIGN.md. LEFT 8/12 holds all content; RIGHT 4/12 completely empty (presenter video). No spacebar hint. In the left region render: (1) Thin 120px terracotta rule (#C8744A) above the eyebrow. (2) Eyebrow in DM Mono 11px uppercase #C0392B: '01 — DISCOVERY'. (3) Headline in Fraunces weight-300 40px off-white: 'Before we build anything, we need to understand the objective.' with natural line breaks. (4) Below the headline, a full-width SVG scene 180px tall. A horizontal baseline spans the full SVG width at y=130, color #2A2724, 1px. Five spacebar presses progressively build the scene: PRESS 1 — fade in a minimal stick figure on the left (circle head, line body, legs, arms, all #F0EBE3 2px stroke) standing on the baseline. Below baseline centered under it: label 'WHERE YOU ARE' in DM Mono 11px #7A7068. PRESS 2 — fade in a simple treasure chest on the right side of the SVG (rectangle body, arc lid, latch circle, stroked #C8744A 2px) on the baseline. Label below: 'WHERE YOU WANT TO GO'. PRESS 3 — break the baseline in the center third (erase that segment) and draw a fire pit in the gap: 3 overlapping flame shapes in #C0392B and #C8744A that flicker with a looping opacity animation (0.7→1.0, 1.2s ease-in-out infinite). Label below gap: 'WHAT'S STOPPING YOU'. PRESS 4 — draw a bridge over the gap: a flat horizontal path at baseline height connecting the two edges, with 2 short vertical supports, stroked #F0EBE3 2px, drawn left-to-right via stroke-dashoffset over 500ms. Label above bridge: 'HOW TO GET THERE' in DM Mono 11px #7A7068. PRESS 5 — overlay 6 small brick rectangles evenly spaced along the bridge surface, each stroked #C8744A 1.5px with fill #3D2418, popping in one by one with 60ms stagger, scale 0→1 over 250ms. Label below bridge: 'WHAT MAKES IT A SOLUTION' in DM Mono 11px #C8744A. Background #0E0D0C. All DESIGN.md tokens apply.

slides_next.title: Discovery — Identifying Bottlenecks

content.headline:
Every workflow has
a breaking point.

flowchart_layout.description: Three horizontal flowcharts stacked vertically. Each represents a different business workflow. All three flowcharts are visible on load but only the first is fully opaque — the 2nd and 3rd start hidden. Each chart has 5 nodes connected by arrows left-to-right. Each node is a rounded rectangle. One node per chart is the bottleneck. Spacebar presses progressively reveal bottlenecks and new charts.

flowcharts.label: Client Onboarding
flowcharts.label_style: DM Mono, 10px, uppercase, #7A7068, left-aligned above chart

nodes.bottleneck_note: Hours spent reading forms and copy-pasting to CRM

bottleneck_label.entrance: fade-in, 250ms ease-out, after node turns red

flowcharts.label: Project Reporting
flowcharts.label_style: DM Mono, 10px, uppercase, #7A7068, left-aligned above chart

nodes.bottleneck_note: Sending individual reports by hand, every week

flowcharts.bottleneck_trigger: spacebar press 2 — revealed simultaneously with chart appearing

flowcharts.label: Invoice & Payment
flowcharts.label_style: DM Mono, 10px, uppercase, #7A7068, left-aligned above chart

nodes.bottleneck_note: Manual invoice drafting delays cash flow by days

flowcharts.bottleneck_trigger: spacebar press 3 — revealed simultaneously with chart appearing

spacebar_sequence.action: Turn bottleneck node red on flow-1 (f1-n3 'Manual Review'). Node fill becomes #2A0A08, stroke becomes #C0392B, text becomes #F0EBE3, glow appears. 'Bottleneck' label fades in below it.
flow-2 (Project Reporting) fades up into view. Simultaneously after 300ms, f2-n4 'Manual Send' turns red with the same bottleneck style. 'Bottleneck' label fades in below it.
flow-3 (Invoice & Payment) fades up into view. Simultaneously after 300ms, f3-n3 'Draft Invoice' turns red. 'Bottleneck' label fades in below it.

slides_next.prompt: Build a single full-viewport HTML slide following DESIGN.md. LEFT 8/12 holds all content; RIGHT 4/12 completely empty (presenter video). No spacebar hint. Left region: (1) Thin 120px terracotta rule (#C8744A). (2) Eyebrow DM Mono 11px uppercase #C0392B: '01 — DISCOVERY'. (3) Headline Fraunces weight-300 40px: 'Every workflow has a breaking point.' (4) Three SVG flowcharts stacked vertically with 20px gaps. Each chart is a horizontal row of 5 rounded-rect nodes (80×34px, rx 6, fill #161513, stroke #2A2724 1.5px) connected by arrows (line + small arrowhead, stroke #2A2724). Node labels in DM Mono 10px uppercase #7A7068. A flow label in DM Mono 10px uppercase #7A7068 sits above-left of each chart. CHART 1 'CLIENT ONBOARDING' — nodes: Inquiry → Intake Form → Manual Review → Contract → Kickoff. Fully visible on load. CHART 2 'PROJECT REPORTING' — nodes: Data Pull → Spreadsheet → Format → Manual Send → Follow-up. Hidden on load (opacity 0). CHART 3 'INVOICE & PAYMENT' — nodes: Job Done → Time Log → Draft Invoice → Approval → Payment. Hidden on load (opacity 0). SPACEBAR INTERACTIONS: Press 1 — node 'Manual Review' (chart 1, index 2) transitions to bottleneck style: fill #2A0A08, stroke #C0392B 2px, text color #F0EBE3, drop-shadow 0 0 8px #C0392B66, 300ms ease-out. A label 'BOTTLENECK' in DM Mono 10px #C0392B fades in below it. Press 2 — chart 2 fades up (translateY 12px→0, opacity 0→1, 400ms ease-out). After 300ms delay, node 'Manual Send' (index 3) turns bottleneck red with same style + label. Press 3 — chart 3 fades up same way. After 300ms, node 'Draft Invoice' (index 2) turns bottleneck red + label. Background #0E0D0C. All DESIGN.md tokens apply.

slides_next.title: Discovery — Prioritizing Bottlenecks

content.headline:
Now we put them
in order.

diagram.description: A two-column SVG layout side by side, filling the content width. LEFT column: 5 bottleneck nodes stacked vertically, each a different shape, all styled as bottleneck-red (fill #2A0A08, stroke #C0392B, glow). No text inside any node. They represent the raw unordered pile of problems found. RIGHT column: same 5 nodes but now arranged vertically numbered 01–05, each a clean default-style rounded rect (fill #161513, stroke #2A2724), numbered in DM Mono to the left of each node. A large single arrow spans horizontally from the left column to the right column through the center of the diagram. The right column and arrow are all hidden on load. Spacebar press 1 reveals everything — the arrow draws left-to-right, then the right column fades up with the numbered nodes staggering in top-to-bottom with 80ms delays.

left_column.label: Bottlenecks Found
left_column.label_style: DM Mono, 10px, uppercase, #7A7068, centered above column

center_arrow.description: A single large horizontal arrow spanning the center of the diagram from left column to right column

right_column.label: Prioritized
right_column.label_style: DM Mono, 10px, uppercase, #7A7068, centered above column

number_label.position: to the left of node, outside

right_column.entrance: each node fades up with 80ms stagger, 300ms ease-out, after arrow finishes drawing

spacebar_sequence.action: Center arrow draws left-to-right over 500ms. Once complete, right column label fades in, then the 5 numbered nodes stagger in top-to-bottom with 80ms delays, each fading up (translateY 8px→0, opacity 0→1, 300ms ease-out). Numbers 01–05 appear in terracotta to the left of each clean node.

slides_next.prompt: Build a single full-viewport HTML slide following DESIGN.md. LEFT 8/12 holds all content; RIGHT 4/12 completely empty (presenter video). No spacebar hint. (3) Headline Fraunces weight-300 40px: 'Now we put them in order.' with a line break after 'them'. (4) Below headline, a wide SVG diagram with two vertical columns side by side connected by a horizontal arrow. All styled as bottleneck: fill #2A0A08, stroke #C0392B 2px, filter drop-shadow 0 0 6px #C0392B55. slides_next.title: Discovery — Solution Roadmap

content.headline:
Here's what we're
going to build.

diagram.description: A horizontal roadmap of 6 solution nodes connected left-to-right by connector lines with arrowheads. All nodes are visible on load in their default state — no labels, no text inside any node, just clean rounded rects. Each subsequent spacebar press stamps a green checkmark over the next node from left to right, signaling completion. The connectors between nodes are always visible.
diagram.layout: single horizontal row, full content width

default.text: none — no labels inside or outside nodes

checked.overlay: green checkmark SVG centered over node

checkmark.style: thin stroke checkmark, stroke-width 2.5px, stroke-linecap round, stroke-linejoin round

spacebar_sequence.description: Each spacebar press checks off the next node left-to-right. 6 nodes = 6 presses.

presses.action: Node 1 border turns green (#27AE60), fill shifts to #0D1F12, checkmark draws in over 300ms
Node 6 same treatment. After a 300ms delay, a subtle label fades in below the entire row: 'Your AI roadmap.' in Fraunces italic weight-300 18px #F0EBE3

spacing.between_nodes: connector lines, ~24px gap between node edges

slides_next.prompt: Build a single full-viewport HTML slide following DESIGN.md. LEFT 8/12 holds all content; RIGHT 4/12 completely empty (presenter video). No spacebar hint. (3) Headline Fraunces weight-300 40px: 'Here's what we're going to build.' with a line break after 'we're'. (4) Below headline, a horizontal row of 6 rounded-rect nodes (68×44px, rx 8, fill #161513, stroke #2A2724 1.5px) connected left-to-right by lines with small filled triangle arrowheads (stroke #2A2724). No text inside or outside any node. All nodes and connectors visible on load. After the 6th node is checked, a label fades in below the row in Fraunces italic weight-300 18px #F0EBE3 reading 'Your AI roadmap.' Background #0E0D0C. All DESIGN.md tokens apply.
